seo.title: Smolagents Guardrails for Fintech
seo.description: Compliance guide for Smolagents agents in fintech. Meet SOX, PCI DSS requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: Fintech teams adopting Smolagents face heightened risks due to the framework's lightweight design, which prioritizes simplicity over built-in safeguards, potentially leading to unencrypted transaction data exposure or non-compliant financial advice generation. Regulatory scrutiny from SOX, PCI DSS, GDPR, SEC, and BSA/AML demands rigorous compliance, where even minor agent errors can trigger severe penalties including SEC enforcement or license revocation. Vex provides essential guardrails by verifying agent outputs against industry-specific policies before execution.

complianceRequirements.description: Requires encryption of transaction data at rest and in transit, prohibiting unencrypted handling of cardholder information.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex scans Smolagents-generated code for encryption usage (e.g., AES-256) and blocks execution of raw data handling tools.

complianceRequirements.description: Mandates data minimization, consent tracking, and right to explanation for automated decisions affecting EU citizens.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex enforces PII redaction policies and requires explainability annotations in multi-step reasoning chains.

complianceRequirements.description: Financial advice from AI must include appropriate disclaimers and avoid unsubstantiated investment recommendations.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex verifies disclaimer presence in advisory outputs and blocks high-risk recommendations without human review.

complianceRequirements.description: Requires transaction monitoring for suspicious patterns and mandatory reporting of potential money laundering.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex validates AML rule implementation in agent tools and flags incomplete transaction pattern analysis.

industryRisks.name: Unencrypted Transaction Processing
industryRisks.description: Smolagents code agents generating raw SQL queries or API calls without TLS/encryption.
industryRisks.example: Agent executes `curl -X POST https://bank-api/pay --data-raw '{card: 4532...}'` without HTTPS verification.

industryRisks.name: Non-Compliant Financial Advice
industryRisks.description: Tool agents recommending investments without SEC-mandated disclaimers or risk warnings.
industryRisks.example: Multi-step reasoning suggests 'Buy TSLA calls immediately' without suitability assessment.

industryRisks.name: Compounding AML False Negatives
industryRisks.description: Multi-step reasoning errors missing suspicious transaction patterns across chained tool calls.
industryRisks.example: Agent splits $9,900 deposit into 10x $990 transactions, evading $10k reporting threshold.

industryRisks.name: Missing Error Handling in Credit Models
industryRisks.description: Generated code lacking try-catch blocks for credit scoring decisions affecting consumers.
industryRisks.example: Division by zero in debt-to-income ratio crashes credit approval workflow.

implementation.title: PCI-Compliant Transaction Wrapper
implementation.description: Wrap smolagents.run() to verify encryption before payment processing tools execute.

implementation.title: SEC Disclaimer Enforcement
implementation.description: Ensure all financial advice includes mandatory risk disclaimers using output verification.
implementation.code:
import vex
from smolagents import Agent

agent = Agent(tools=[investment_analyzer])

@vex.policy()
def sec_disclaimer_check(output: str) -> bool:
    disclaimer = 'NOT FINANCIAL ADVICE. Past performance ≠ future results.'
    return disclaimer in output

response = vex.verify(
    lambda: agent.run('Analyze AAPL for investment'),
    policies=[sec_disclaimer_check]
)
print(response.output if response.verdict == 'pass' else 'Blocked: Missing SEC disclaimer')

implementation.title: AML Pattern Detection Guardrail
implementation.description: Block multi-step reasoning that fragments transactions to evade reporting thresholds.

caseStudyScenario.heading: Smolagents in Fintech: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario:
NeoBank deploys Smolagents for real-time transaction categorization and investment recommendations. The lightweight framework enables rapid prototyping, with code agents generating SQL queries for transaction data and tool agents calling payment processors. During beta testing, the system processes $2M daily volume, but regulators flag issues: unencrypted API calls to payment gateways, investment suggestions lacking SEC disclaimers, and multi-step reasoning splitting large deposits into sub-$10k transactions evading AML monitoring.

Without guardrails, a production incident occurs when a code agent generates raw curl commands exposing 15,000 customer card numbers. SEC investigates after viral social media posts show the AI recommending high-risk options to retail investors without risk warnings. The bank faces $8M in potential fines and remediation costs.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: Implementing Vex verification reduced compliance violations by 97%. Transaction tools now require TLS 1.3 and AES-256 encryption, verified before execution. All investment outputs automatically append SEC-compliant disclaimers. AML policies block structuring patterns, flagging 23 suspicious deposit patterns in the first month. The verified Smolagents deployment passed independent PCI DSS audit, avoiding $5.2M in penalties while maintaining sub-200ms response times.

cta.heading: Comply with Fintech Regulations Using Vex
cta.description: Install Vex today with `pip install vex-sdk` and wrap your Smolagents deployments to eliminate compliance risks.
